'Ndrina D'Agostino.description: The D'Agostino 'ndrina is a powerful Calabrian Mafia clan based in Rosarno, Laureana di Borrello, Sant'Ilario dello Ionio, and Canolo, Italy. It operates in drug trafficking and has been historically important within the 'Ndrangheta, serving as a link between Reggio and Locri factions. The clan has been involved in feuds and is currently largely incarcerated but still influential.

It was registered as a designated yakuza group under the Organized Crime Countermeasures Law in May 1993.

Fatemi Circle.description: A clique of statesmen within the Government of Mahmoud Ahmadinejad, based in Tehran, Iran, founded around 2005. The group was led by alleged head Mohammad-Reza Rahimi, and many members were arrested or jailed.

Islamic State – Somalia Province.description: Islamic State affiliate operating primarily in the mountainous regions of Puntland, northern Somalia. Formed in late 2015 when pro-IS fighters defected from al-Shabaab under Abdul Qadir Mumin. Recognized by IS leadership as Somalia Province in December 2017. Engages in attacks against Puntland security forces and is in conflict with al-Shabaab. Estimated 100–700 fighters, with foreign fighters possibly outnumbering Somali members as of 2024.

Lucchese crime family.description: The Lucchese crime family is one of the Five Families of the American Mafia, based in New York City and New Jersey. Founded in the early 1920s, it was originally led by Gaetano Reina and later Tommy Gagliano. Under Tommy Lucchese, the family became one of the most powerful on the Mafia Commission. It is currently active, with involvement in racketeering, gambling, loansharking, and drug trafficking.

San Francisco crime family.description: Italian American Mafia crime family based in San Francisco, California, organized in the early 1930s by Francesco 'Frank' Lanza. The family was active primarily in the San Francisco Bay Area and Northern California, involved in racketeering, gambling, loansharking, and narcotics trafficking. Membership peaked at 15-20 made men under boss James 'Jimmy the Hat' Lanza. The family became defunct around 2006.
